mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25% and remains there for at least 2 months, the zero lower bound constraint becomes binding, signaling exhausted conventional monetary policy. Equity investors interpret this as a regime of financial repression and reduced policy optionality, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as risk premiums expand.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of June 2015 indicates ZLB regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window post-2008.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) of more than 50,000 persons signals deteriorating labor market momentum. This triggers a negative equity risk premium adjustment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice recession probability.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or correlation between PAYEMS monthly change and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates to below 2% year-over-year while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) fall below 600,000 annualized units, aggregate demand weakness is confirmed across both consumption and investment channels. This joint signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth < 2% AND HOUST < 600k annualized units
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 574k as of May 2015 indicates weakness
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction across consumption and investment
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) exhibits a 3-month moving average decline of more than 0.5% from its prior 12-month peak, signaling supply-side contraction and reduced capacity utilization. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as profit margins compress and cyclical earnings disappoint.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in < 58% of triggered instances, or Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO 3-month change and 20-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.08).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth rate falls below 1.5% while the federal funds rate remains above 0.10%, real interest rates turn positive and restrictive. This combination signals monetary policy is tightening in real terms, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations are compressed.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.541 and FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of June 2015
regime_hint: real monetary tightening with low inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 62% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL YoY) produces Rank IC < 0.035 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month rolling window, signaling accelerating labor market slack. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprrice earnings risk and recession probability increases.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 10.0% as of June 2015 indicates elevated slack
regime_hint: labor market deterioration and rising unemployment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in < 56% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE 3-month change and 20-day forward returns is < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter annualized rate falls below 1.5%, indicating sub-trend economic expansion. This always-on signal predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices terminal growth expectations and equity risk premiums widen.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 58% of all observations with GDPC1 QoQ annualized < 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) both decline simultaneously over a 2-month window, indicating synchronized contraction across supply capacity and labor input. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors recognize broad-based economic weakness.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.3% AND PAYEMS declines > 30k over 2-month window
evidence_refs: fact:Both series show recent stability as of June 2015
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in < 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (INDPRO change × PAYEMS change) produces Rank IC < 0.038 over any 2-year rolling window.
